pr.title: Bring CObjGetUpVector closer to a match
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 2635 made a focused decomp-matching cleanup in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.c`: inside `HSD_CObjGetLeftVector`, a duplicated inline reconstruction of up-vector selection and fallback initialization was replaced with a direct call to `HSD_CObjGetUpVector(cobj, &up)`. The bot report recorded `HSD_CObjGetLeftVector` improving from 33.92% to 98.65%, but the evidence does not show a full match.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; updates `HSD_CObjGetLeftVector` to call `HSD_CObjGetUpVector` instead of carrying duplicated local up-vector logic.

what_changed.text: The removed code manually checked `cobj->flags & 1`, copied `cobj->u.up`, called `roll2upvec(cobj, &up, cobj->u.roll)`, and otherwise initialized `up` to `(0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f)` with `res = -1`.
The change was one hunk in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.c` with 1 added line and 16 deleted lines.
The automated report recorded one improvement for `main/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj` item `HSD_CObjGetLeftVector`, from 33.92% before to 98.65% after.

smart_moves.text: Reused an existing semantic helper (`HSD_CObjGetUpVector`) rather than preserving duplicated generated-looking control flow inside `HSD_CObjGetLeftVector`.
Kept the transformation narrow: the PR touched only one function area in one file and did not introduce data, header, or symbol churn.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function locally duplicates the behavior of an existing helper, trying the helper call can both improve readability and substantially improve the match score.
Do not assume an inlined reconstruction of flag checks and fallback vector initialization is original source when a nearby named API function expresses the same operation.

naming_conventions.text: No new naming convention is established. Existing names in the evidence include `HSD_CObjGetLeftVector`, `HSD_CObjGetUpVector`, `HSD_CObjGetEyeVector`, `roll2upvec`, `vec_normalize_check`, `eye`, `up`, and `left`.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace duplicated local logic with an existing helper call to alter codegen toward the target.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.result_supported_by_pr: Automated report shows `HSD_CObjGetLeftVector` improved from 33.92% to 98.65%; no full match is shown.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Avoided adding pragmas, register steering, dummy locals, data anchors, or literal/symbol churn in this slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.result_supported_by_pr: The diff shows only the helper-call replacement.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 recorded 1 improvement in an unmatched item: `main/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj` / `HSD_CObjGetLeftVector`, +412 bytes, 33.92% before, 98.65% after.

follow_up_queries: roll2upvec cobj flags u.up fallback vector

notes: Strong evidence for the exact file and code change from the loaded diff.
Strong evidence for score movement from the automated bot report.
PR body is empty and there are no review comments, so there is no direct author explanation or human reviewer rationale.
The title says closer to a match and the bot report shows 98.65%, so this intake does not claim the function fully matched.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer existing helper calls over duplicated reconstructed logic when the helper expresses the same local operation
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2635, `HSD_CObjGetLeftVector` improved substantially after replacing a duplicated local up-vector reconstruction with `HSD_CObjGetUpVector(cobj, &up)`. This is a graph-safe candidate lesson for helper reuse in decomp matching, scoped to the CObj vector-helper context.

rejection_notes: Do not promote a full-match claim: the available evidence only reports improvement to 98.65%.
Do not infer reviewer intent or acceptance rationale beyond merge metadata and the bot report; no human review comments are present.
Do not generalize this into a global rule that helper calls are always preferred over inlining; this PR only supports a scoped helper-reuse tactic for this CObj vector case.
